#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Deploy Changelogue to Azure App Service (Container).
# Idempotent — safe to re-run at any point.
#
# Usage:
#   # First-time setup (creates all resources):
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh setup
#
#   # Redeploy after code changes:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh deploy
#
#   # Tear down everything:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh destroy
#
# Required environment variables (or set in .env):
#   AZURE_DB_PASSWORD   - PostgreSQL admin password
#
# Optional environment variables:
#   AZURE_LOCATION      - Azure region (default: westus)
#   AZURE_RG            - Resource group name (default: changelogue-res)
#   AZURE_ACR           - ACR name (default: changelogueacr)
#   AZURE_DB_SERVER     - Postgres server name (default: changelogue-db)
#   AZURE_APP_PLAN      - App Service plan name (default: changelogue-plan)
#   AZURE_APP_NAME      - Web app name (default: changelogue-app)
#   AZURE_APP_SKU       - App Service SKU (default: F1)
#   GOOGLE_API_KEY      - For AI features (optional)
#   NO_AUTH             - Skip API auth (default: true)

set -euo pipefail

# Load .env if present
if [[ -f .env ]]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
  source .env
fi

# --- Configuration ---
LOCATION="${AZURE_LOCATION:-westus}"
RG="${AZURE_RG:-changelogue-res}"
ACR="${AZURE_ACR:-changelogueacr}"
DB_SERVER="${AZURE_DB_SERVER:-changelogue-db}"
DB_NAME="changelogue"
DB_USER="pgadmin"
DB_PASSWORD="${AZURE_DB_PASSWORD:-}"
APP_PLAN="${AZURE_APP_PLAN:-changelogue-plan}"
APP_NAME="${AZURE_APP_NAME:-changelogue-app}"
APP_SKU="${AZURE_APP_SKU:-F1}"
IMAGE="${ACR}.azurecr.io/changelogue:latest"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log()   { echo -e "${GREEN}[+]${NC} $*"; }
warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $*"; }
skip()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[=]${NC} $* (already exists, skipping)"; }
error() { echo -e "${RED}[x]${NC} $*" >&2; exit 1; }

require_password() {
  if [[ -z "$DB_PASSWORD" ]]; then
    error "AZURE_DB_PASSWORD is required. Set it in .env or export it."
  fi
}

# Check if an Azure resource exists. Returns 0 if it does.
resource_exists() {
  local type="$1"
  shift
  case "$type" in
    rg)
      az group show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr)
      az acr show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres)
      az postgres flexible-server show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres-db)
      az postgres flexible-server db show --resource-group "$1" --server-name "$2" --database-name "$3" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    plan)
      az appservice plan show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    webapp)
      az webapp show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
  esac
}

# --- Commands ---

cmd_setup() {
  require_password

  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  # Resource group
  if resource_exists rg "$RG"; then
    skip "Resource group: $RG"
  else
    log "Creating resource group: $RG"
    az group create --name "$RG" --location "$LOCATION" --output none
  fi

  # Container Registry
  if resource_exists acr "$ACR"; then
    skip "Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
  else
    log "Creating Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
    az acr create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$ACR" \
      --sku Basic \
      --admin-enabled true \
      --output none
  fi

  # PostgreSQL Flexible Server
  if resource_exists postgres "$RG" "$DB_SERVER"; then
    skip "PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
  else
    log "Creating PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
    az postgres flexible-server create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --admin-user "$DB_USER" \
      --admin-password "$DB_PASSWORD" \
      --sku-name Standard_B1ms \
      --tier Burstable \
      --version 16 \
      --public-access 0.0.0.0 \
      --output none \
      --yes
  fi

  # Database
  if resource_exists postgres-db "$RG" "$DB_SERVER" "$DB_NAME"; then
    skip "Database: $DB_NAME"
  else
    log "Creating database: $DB_NAME"
    az postgres flexible-server db create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --server-name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --database-name "$DB_NAME" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Build container image (always rebuild — this is the deploy step)
  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  # App Service plan
  if resource_exists plan "$RG" "$APP_PLAN"; then
    skip "App Service plan: $APP_PLAN"
  else
    log "Creating App Service plan: $APP_PLAN ($APP_SKU)"
    az appservice plan create \
      --name "$APP_PLAN" \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --sku "$APP_SKU" \
      --is-linux \
      --output none
  fi

  # Web App
  if resource_exists webapp "$RG" "$APP_NAME"; then
    skip "Web App: $APP_NAME"
    log "Updating container image..."
  else
    log "Creating Web App: $APP_NAME"
    az webapp create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --plan "$APP_PLAN" \
      --name "$APP_NAME" \
      --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
      --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Always update ACR credentials and container config
  log "Configuring ACR credentials for App Service..."
  ACR_PASSWORD=$(az acr credential show --name "$ACR" --query "passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
  az webapp config container set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
    --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
    --container-registry-user "$ACR" \
    --container-registry-password "$ACR_PASSWORD" \
    --output none

  DB_HOST="${DB_SERVER}.postgres.database.azure.com"
  DATABASE_URL="postgres://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST}:5432/${DB_NAME}?sslmode=require"

  # Always update app settings
  log "Setting app configuration..."
  SETTINGS="DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL LISTEN_ADDR=:8080 WEBSITES_PORT=8080 NO_AUTH=${NO_AUTH:-true}"
  if [[ -n "${GOOGLE_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS GOOGLE_API_KEY=$GOOGLE_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_PROVIDER:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_PROVIDER=$LLM_PROVIDER"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_MODEL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_MODEL=$LLM_MODEL"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_API_KEY=$OPENAI_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_BASE_URL=$OPENAI_BASE_URL"
  fi
  az webapp config appsettings set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --settings $SETTINGS \
    --output none

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Setup complete!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
  echo "  API:     https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net/api/v1/health"
  echo ""
  echo "  To redeploy after code changes: $0 deploy"
}

cmd_deploy() {
  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Deployed!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
}

cmd_destroy() {
  warn "This will delete ALL resources in resource group: $RG"
  read -rp "Type the resource group name to confirm: " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" != "$RG" ]]; then
    error "Aborted."
  fi

  log "Deleting resource group: $RG (this may take a few minutes)..."
  az group delete --name "$RG" --yes --no-wait

  log "Deletion initiated. Resources will be removed in the background."
}

cmd_logs() {
  log "Streaming logs from $APP_NAME..."
  az webapp log tail --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"
}

# --- Main ---
case "${1:-}" in
  setup)   cmd_setup ;;
  deploy)  cmd_deploy ;;
  destroy) cmd_destroy ;;
  logs)    cmd_logs ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {setup|deploy|destroy|logs}"
    echo ""
    echo "  setup   - Create all Azure resources and deploy (idempotent)"
    echo "  deploy  - Rebuild image and restart (after code changes)"
    echo "  destroy - Delete all Azure resources"
    echo "  logs    - Stream app logs"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
  echo "  API:     https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net/api/v1/health"
  echo ""
  echo "  To redeploy after code changes: $0 deploy"
}

cmd_deploy() {
  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Deployed!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
}

cmd_destroy() {
  warn "This will delete ALL resources in resource group: $RG"
  read -rp "Type the resource group name to confirm: " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" != "$RG" ]]; then
    error "Aborted."
  fi

  log "Deleting resource group: $RG (this may take a few minutes)..."
  az group delete --name "$RG" --yes --no-wait

  log "Deletion initiated. Resources will be removed in the background."
}

cmd_logs() {
  log "Streaming logs from $APP_NAME..."
  az webapp log tail --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"
}

# --- Main ---
case "${1:-}" in
  setup)   cmd_setup ;;
  deploy)  cmd_deploy ;;
  destroy) cmd_destroy ;;
  logs)    cmd_logs ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {setup|deploy|destroy|logs}"
    echo ""
    echo "  setup   - Create all Azure resources and deploy"
    echo "  deploy  - Rebuild image and restart (after code changes)"
    echo "  destroy - Delete all Azure resources"
    echo "  logs    - Stream app logs"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

content.text:
Done. The script is now idempotent — you can re-run `./scripts/azure-deploy.sh setup` safely. Changes:

1. **SKU default changed to `F1`** (free tier) — no VM quota needed
2. **Every resource creation checks if it already exists first** — skips with `[=] ... (already exists, skipping)` instead of failing
3. **Always-update steps** (image build, ACR credentials, app settings, restart) still run every time since those are the "deploy" part

Your existing resource group, ACR, PostgreSQL server, and database will all be skipped. It will pick up from the App Service plan creation (now F1) onward. Go ahead and re-run it:

```bash
./scripts/azure-deploy.sh setup
```

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Deploy Changelogue to Azure App Service (Container).
# Idempotent — safe to re-run at any point.
#
# Usage:
#   # First-time setup (creates all resources):
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh setup
#
#   # Redeploy after code changes:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh deploy
#
#   # Tear down everything:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh destroy
#
# Required environment variables (or set in .env):
#   AZURE_DB_PASSWORD   - PostgreSQL admin password
#
# Optional environment variables:
#   AZURE_LOCATION      - Azure region (default: westus)
#   AZURE_RG            - Resource group name (default: changelogue-res)
#   AZURE_ACR           - ACR name (default: changelogueacr)
#   AZURE_DB_SERVER     - Postgres server name (default: changelogue-db)
#   AZURE_APP_PLAN      - App Service plan name (default: changelogue-plan)
#   AZURE_APP_NAME      - Web app name (default: changelogue-app)
#   AZURE_APP_SKU       - App Service SKU (default: F1)
#   GOOGLE_API_KEY      - For AI features (optional)
#   NO_AUTH             - Skip API auth (default: true)

set -euo pipefail

# Load .env if present
if [[ -f .env ]]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
  source .env
fi

# --- Configuration ---
LOCATION="${AZURE_LOCATION:-westus}"
RG="${AZURE_RG:-changelogue-res}"
ACR="${AZURE_ACR:-changelogueacr}"
DB_SERVER="${AZURE_DB_SERVER:-changelogue-db}"
DB_NAME="changelogue"
DB_USER="pgadmin"
DB_PASSWORD="${AZURE_DB_PASSWORD:-}"
APP_PLAN="${AZURE_APP_PLAN:-changelogue-plan}"
APP_NAME="${AZURE_APP_NAME:-changelogue-app}"
APP_SKU="${AZURE_APP_SKU:-F1}"
IMAGE="${ACR}.azurecr.io/changelogue:latest"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log()   { echo -e "${GREEN}[+]${NC} $*"; }
warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $*"; }
skip()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[=]${NC} $* (already exists, skipping)"; }
error() { echo -e "${RED}[x]${NC} $*" >&2; exit 1; }

require_password() {
  if [[ -z "$DB_PASSWORD" ]]; then
    error "AZURE_DB_PASSWORD is required. Set it in .env or export it."
  fi
}

# Check if an Azure resource exists. Returns 0 if it does.
resource_exists() {
  local type="$1"
  shift
  case "$type" in
    rg)
      az group show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr)
      az acr show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres)
      az postgres flexible-server show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres-db)
      az postgres flexible-server db show --resource-group "$1" --server-name "$2" --database-name "$3" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    plan)
      az appservice plan show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    webapp)
      az webapp show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
  esac
}

# --- Commands ---

cmd_setup() {
  require_password

  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  # Resource group
  if resource_exists rg "$RG"; then
    skip "Resource group: $RG"
  else
    log "Creating resource group: $RG"
    az group create --name "$RG" --location "$LOCATION" --output none
  fi

  # Container Registry
  if resource_exists acr "$ACR"; then
    skip "Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
  else
    log "Creating Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
    az acr create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$ACR" \
      --sku Basic \
      --admin-enabled true \
      --output none
  fi

  # PostgreSQL Flexible Server
  if resource_exists postgres "$RG" "$DB_SERVER"; then
    skip "PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
  else
    log "Creating PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
    az postgres flexible-server create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --admin-user "$DB_USER" \
      --admin-password "$DB_PASSWORD" \
      --sku-name Standard_B1ms \
      --tier Burstable \
      --version 16 \
      --public-access 0.0.0.0 \
      --output none \
      --yes
  fi

  # Database
  if resource_exists postgres-db "$RG" "$DB_SERVER" "$DB_NAME"; then
    skip "Database: $DB_NAME"
  else
    log "Creating database: $DB_NAME"
    az postgres flexible-server db create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --server-name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --database-name "$DB_NAME" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Build container image (always rebuild — this is the deploy step)
  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  # App Service plan
  if resource_exists plan "$RG" "$APP_PLAN"; then
    skip "App Service plan: $APP_PLAN"
  else
    log "Creating App Service plan: $APP_PLAN ($APP_SKU)"
    az appservice plan create \
      --name "$APP_PLAN" \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --sku "$APP_SKU" \
      --is-linux \
      --output none
  fi

  # Web App
  if resource_exists webapp "$RG" "$APP_NAME"; then
    skip "Web App: $APP_NAME"
    log "Updating container image..."
  else
    log "Creating Web App: $APP_NAME"
    az webapp create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --plan "$APP_PLAN" \
      --name "$APP_NAME" \
      --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
      --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Always update ACR credentials and container config
  log "Configuring ACR credentials for App Service..."
  ACR_PASSWORD=$(az acr credential show --name "$ACR" --query "passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
  az webapp config container set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
    --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
    --container-registry-user "$ACR" \
    --container-registry-password "$ACR_PASSWORD" \
    --output none

  DB_HOST="${DB_SERVER}.postgres.database.azure.com"
  DATABASE_URL="postgres://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST}:5432/${DB_NAME}?sslmode=require"

  # Always update app settings
  log "Setting app configuration..."
  SETTINGS="DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL LISTEN_ADDR=:8080 WEBSITES_PORT=8080 NO_AUTH=${NO_AUTH:-true}"
  if [[ -n "${GOOGLE_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS GOOGLE_API_KEY=$GOOGLE_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_PROVIDER:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_PROVIDER=$LLM_PROVIDER"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_MODEL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_MODEL=$LLM_MODEL"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_API_KEY=$OPENAI_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_BASE_URL=$OPENAI_BASE_URL"
  fi
  az webapp config appsettings set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --settings $SETTINGS \
    --output none

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Setup complete!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
  echo "  API:     https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net/api/v1/health"
  echo ""
  echo "  To redeploy after code changes: $0 deploy"
}

cmd_deploy() {
  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Deployed!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
}

cmd_destroy() {
  warn "This will delete ALL resources in resource group: $RG"
  read -rp "Type the resource group name to confirm: " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" != "$RG" ]]; then
    error "Aborted."
  fi

  log "Deleting resource group: $RG (this may take a few minutes)..."
  az group delete --name "$RG" --yes --no-wait

  log "Deletion initiated. Resources will be removed in the background."
}

cmd_logs() {
  log "Streaming logs from $APP_NAME..."
  az webapp log tail --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"
}

# --- Main ---
case "${1:-}" in
  setup)   cmd_setup ;;
  deploy)  cmd_deploy ;;
  destroy) cmd_destroy ;;
  logs)    cmd_logs ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {setup|deploy|destroy|logs}"
    echo ""
    echo "  setup   - Create all Azure resources and deploy (idempotent)"
    echo "  deploy  - Rebuild image and restart (after code changes)"
    echo "  destroy - Delete all Azure resources"
    echo "  logs    - Stream app logs"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

      az webapp show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null

      az webapp show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr-image)
      az acr repository show --name "$1" --image "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Deploy Changelogue to Azure App Service (Container).
# Idempotent — safe to re-run at any point.
#
# Usage:
#   # First-time setup (creates all resources):
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh setup
#
#   # Redeploy after code changes:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh deploy
#
#   # Tear down everything:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh destroy
#
# Required environment variables (or set in .env):
#   AZURE_DB_PASSWORD   - PostgreSQL admin password
#
# Optional environment variables:
#   AZURE_LOCATION      - Azure region (default: westus)
#   AZURE_RG            - Resource group name (default: changelogue-res)
#   AZURE_ACR           - ACR name (default: changelogueacr)
#   AZURE_DB_SERVER     - Postgres server name (default: changelogue-db)
#   AZURE_APP_PLAN      - App Service plan name (default: changelogue-plan)
#   AZURE_APP_NAME      - Web app name (default: changelogue-app)
#   AZURE_APP_SKU       - App Service SKU (default: F1)
#   GOOGLE_API_KEY      - For AI features (optional)
#   NO_AUTH             - Skip API auth (default: true)

set -euo pipefail

# Load .env if present
if [[ -f .env ]]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
  source .env
fi

# --- Configuration ---
LOCATION="${AZURE_LOCATION:-westus}"
RG="${AZURE_RG:-changelogue-res}"
ACR="${AZURE_ACR:-changelogueacr}"
DB_SERVER="${AZURE_DB_SERVER:-changelogue-db}"
DB_NAME="changelogue"
DB_USER="pgadmin"
DB_PASSWORD="${AZURE_DB_PASSWORD:-}"
APP_PLAN="${AZURE_APP_PLAN:-changelogue-plan}"
APP_NAME="${AZURE_APP_NAME:-changelogue-app}"
APP_SKU="${AZURE_APP_SKU:-F1}"
IMAGE="${ACR}.azurecr.io/changelogue:latest"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log()   { echo -e "${GREEN}[+]${NC} $*"; }
warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $*"; }
skip()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[=]${NC} $* (already exists, skipping)"; }
error() { echo -e "${RED}[x]${NC} $*" >&2; exit 1; }

require_password() {
  if [[ -z "$DB_PASSWORD" ]]; then
    error "AZURE_DB_PASSWORD is required. Set it in .env or export it."
  fi
}

# Check if an Azure resource exists. Returns 0 if it does.
resource_exists() {
  local type="$1"
  shift
  case "$type" in
    rg)
      az group show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr)
      az acr show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres)
      az postgres flexible-server show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres-db)
      az postgres flexible-server db show --resource-group "$1" --server-name "$2" --database-name "$3" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    plan)
      az appservice plan show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    webapp)
      az webapp show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
  esac
}

# --- Commands ---

cmd_setup() {
  require_password

  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  # Resource group
  if resource_exists rg "$RG"; then
    skip "Resource group: $RG"
  else
    log "Creating resource group: $RG"
    az group create --name "$RG" --location "$LOCATION" --output none
  fi

  # Container Registry
  if resource_exists acr "$ACR"; then
    skip "Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
  else
    log "Creating Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
    az acr create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$ACR" \
      --sku Basic \
      --admin-enabled true \
      --output none
  fi

  # PostgreSQL Flexible Server
  if resource_exists postgres "$RG" "$DB_SERVER"; then
    skip "PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
  else
    log "Creating PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
    az postgres flexible-server create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --admin-user "$DB_USER" \
      --admin-password "$DB_PASSWORD" \
      --sku-name Standard_B1ms \
      --tier Burstable \
      --version 16 \
      --public-access 0.0.0.0 \
      --output none \
      --yes
  fi

  # Database
  if resource_exists postgres-db "$RG" "$DB_SERVER" "$DB_NAME"; then
    skip "Database: $DB_NAME"
  else
    log "Creating database: $DB_NAME"
    az postgres flexible-server db create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --server-name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --database-name "$DB_NAME" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Build container image (always rebuild — this is the deploy step)
  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  # App Service plan
  if resource_exists plan "$RG" "$APP_PLAN"; then
    skip "App Service plan: $APP_PLAN"
  else
    log "Creating App Service plan: $APP_PLAN ($APP_SKU)"
    az appservice plan create \
      --name "$APP_PLAN" \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --sku "$APP_SKU" \
      --is-linux \
      --output none
  fi

  # Web App
  if resource_exists webapp "$RG" "$APP_NAME"; then
    skip "Web App: $APP_NAME"
    log "Updating container image..."
  else
    log "Creating Web App: $APP_NAME"
    az webapp create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --plan "$APP_PLAN" \
      --name "$APP_NAME" \
      --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
      --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Always update ACR credentials and container config
  log "Configuring ACR credentials for App Service..."
  ACR_PASSWORD=$(az acr credential show --name "$ACR" --query "passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
  az webapp config container set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
    --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
    --container-registry-user "$ACR" \
    --container-registry-password "$ACR_PASSWORD" \
    --output none

  DB_HOST="${DB_SERVER}.postgres.database.azure.com"
  DATABASE_URL="postgres://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST}:5432/${DB_NAME}?sslmode=require"

  # Always update app settings
  log "Setting app configuration..."
  SETTINGS="DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL LISTEN_ADDR=:8080 WEBSITES_PORT=8080 NO_AUTH=${NO_AUTH:-true}"
  if [[ -n "${GOOGLE_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS GOOGLE_API_KEY=$GOOGLE_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_PROVIDER:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_PROVIDER=$LLM_PROVIDER"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_MODEL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_MODEL=$LLM_MODEL"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_API_KEY=$OPENAI_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_BASE_URL=$OPENAI_BASE_URL"
  fi
  az webapp config appsettings set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --settings $SETTINGS \
    --output none

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Setup complete!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
  echo "  API:     https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net/api/v1/health"
  echo ""
  echo "  To redeploy after code changes: $0 deploy"
}

cmd_deploy() {
  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Deployed!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
}

cmd_destroy() {
  warn "This will delete ALL resources in resource group: $RG"
  read -rp "Type the resource group name to confirm: " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" != "$RG" ]]; then
    error "Aborted."
  fi

  log "Deleting resource group: $RG (this may take a few minutes)..."
  az group delete --name "$RG" --yes --no-wait

  log "Deletion initiated. Resources will be removed in the background."
}

cmd_logs() {
  log "Streaming logs from $APP_NAME..."
  az webapp log tail --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"
}

# --- Main ---
case "${1:-}" in
  setup)   cmd_setup ;;
  deploy)  cmd_deploy ;;
  destroy) cmd_destroy ;;
  logs)    cmd_logs ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {setup|deploy|destroy|logs}"
    echo ""
    echo "  setup   - Create all Azure resources and deploy (idempotent)"
    echo "  deploy  - Rebuild image and restart (after code changes)"
    echo "  destroy - Delete all Azure resources"
    echo "  logs    - Stream app logs"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

content.text:
Done. Now during `setup`, the image build is skipped if `changelogue:latest` already exists in ACR. The `deploy` command still always rebuilds (that's its purpose — push code changes).

To force a rebuild during setup, you can use `deploy` instead, or delete the image first with:
```bash
az acr repository delete --name changelogueacr --image changelogue:latest --yes
```

content.text:
Several regions work. But your PostgreSQL is already in `westus` — App Service can connect to Postgres cross-region, but it adds latency. Let me check if `westus` itself failed and if any of the Postgres-available regions also support F1. 

Cross-referencing: regions with **both** unrestricted Postgres and F1 quota: `westus3`, `centralus`, `northcentralus`, `canadacentral`.

The simplest fix: the App Service plan doesn't need to be in the same region as Postgres. Let me update the script to support a separate location for the App Service plan. 

Actually, the cleaner approach: just put the **App Service plan in a region that works** while keeping Postgres where it is. Let me clean up the test plan that got created, then update the script.

input.command: az appservice plan delete --name test-plan-check --resource-group changelogue-res --yes --output none 2>/dev/null; echo "cleaned up"

content.content: cleaned up

toolUseResult.oldString:
#   AZURE_APP_SKU       - App Service SKU (default: F1)
#   GOOGLE_API_KEY      - For AI features (optional)
toolUseResult.newString:
#   AZURE_APP_SKU       - App Service SKU (default: F1)
#   AZURE_APP_LOCATION  - App Service region, if different from AZURE_LOCATION
#   GOOGLE_API_KEY      - For AI features (optional)
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Deploy Changelogue to Azure App Service (Container).
# Idempotent — safe to re-run at any point.
#
# Usage:
#   # First-time setup (creates all resources):
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh setup
#
#   # Redeploy after code changes:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh deploy
#
#   # Tear down everything:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh destroy
#
# Required environment variables (or set in .env):
#   AZURE_DB_PASSWORD   - PostgreSQL admin password
#
# Optional environment variables:
#   AZURE_LOCATION      - Azure region (default: westus)
#   AZURE_RG            - Resource group name (default: changelogue-res)
#   AZURE_ACR           - ACR name (default: changelogueacr)
#   AZURE_DB_SERVER     - Postgres server name (default: changelogue-db)
#   AZURE_APP_PLAN      - App Service plan name (default: changelogue-plan)
#   AZURE_APP_NAME      - Web app name (default: changelogue-app)
#   AZURE_APP_SKU       - App Service SKU (default: F1)
#   GOOGLE_API_KEY      - For AI features (optional)
#   NO_AUTH             - Skip API auth (default: true)

set -euo pipefail

# Load .env if present
if [[ -f .env ]]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
  source .env
fi

# --- Configuration ---
LOCATION="${AZURE_LOCATION:-westus}"
RG="${AZURE_RG:-changelogue-res}"
ACR="${AZURE_ACR:-changelogueacr}"
DB_SERVER="${AZURE_DB_SERVER:-changelogue-db}"
DB_NAME="changelogue"
DB_USER="pgadmin"
DB_PASSWORD="${AZURE_DB_PASSWORD:-}"
APP_PLAN="${AZURE_APP_PLAN:-changelogue-plan}"
APP_NAME="${AZURE_APP_NAME:-changelogue-app}"
APP_SKU="${AZURE_APP_SKU:-F1}"
IMAGE="${ACR}.azurecr.io/changelogue:latest"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log()   { echo -e "${GREEN}[+]${NC} $*"; }
warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $*"; }
skip()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[=]${NC} $* (already exists, skipping)"; }
error() { echo -e "${RED}[x]${NC} $*" >&2; exit 1; }

require_password() {
  if [[ -z "$DB_PASSWORD" ]]; then
    error "AZURE_DB_PASSWORD is required. Set it in .env or export it."
  fi
}

# Check if an Azure resource exists. Returns 0 if it does.
resource_exists() {
  local type="$1"
  shift
  case "$type" in
    rg)
      az group show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr)
      az acr show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres)
      az postgres flexible-server show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres-db)
      az postgres flexible-server db show --resource-group "$1" --server-name "$2" --database-name "$3" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    plan)
      az appservice plan show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    webapp)
      az webapp show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr-image)
      az acr repository show --name "$1" --image "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
  esac
}

# --- Commands ---

cmd_setup() {
  require_password

  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  # Resource group
  if resource_exists rg "$RG"; then
    skip "Resource group: $RG"
  else
    log "Creating resource group: $RG"
    az group create --name "$RG" --location "$LOCATION" --output none
  fi

  # Container Registry
  if resource_exists acr "$ACR"; then
    skip "Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
  else
    log "Creating Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
    az acr create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$ACR" \
      --sku Basic \
      --admin-enabled true \
      --output none
  fi

  # PostgreSQL Flexible Server
  if resource_exists postgres "$RG" "$DB_SERVER"; then
    skip "PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
  else
    log "Creating PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
    az postgres flexible-server create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --admin-user "$DB_USER" \
      --admin-password "$DB_PASSWORD" \
      --sku-name Standard_B1ms \
      --tier Burstable \
      --version 16 \
      --public-access 0.0.0.0 \
      --output none \
      --yes
  fi

  # Database
  if resource_exists postgres-db "$RG" "$DB_SERVER" "$DB_NAME"; then
    skip "Database: $DB_NAME"
  else
    log "Creating database: $DB_NAME"
    az postgres flexible-server db create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --server-name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --database-name "$DB_NAME" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Build container image (skip if image already exists in ACR)
  if resource_exists acr-image "$ACR" "changelogue:latest"; then
    skip "Container image: changelogue:latest"
  else
    log "Building container image in ACR..."
    az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs
  fi

  # App Service plan
  if resource_exists plan "$RG" "$APP_PLAN"; then
    skip "App Service plan: $APP_PLAN"
  else
    log "Creating App Service plan: $APP_PLAN ($APP_SKU)"
    az appservice plan create \
      --name "$APP_PLAN" \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --sku "$APP_SKU" \
      --is-linux \
      --output none
  fi

  # Web App
  if resource_exists webapp "$RG" "$APP_NAME"; then
    skip "Web App: $APP_NAME"
    log "Updating container image..."
  else
    log "Creating Web App: $APP_NAME"
    az webapp create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --plan "$APP_PLAN" \
      --name "$APP_NAME" \
      --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
      --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Always update ACR credentials and container config
  log "Configuring ACR credentials for App Service..."
  ACR_PASSWORD=$(az acr credential show --name "$ACR" --query "passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
  az webapp config container set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
    --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
    --container-registry-user "$ACR" \
    --container-registry-password "$ACR_PASSWORD" \
    --output none

  DB_HOST="${DB_SERVER}.postgres.database.azure.com"
  DATABASE_URL="postgres://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST}:5432/${DB_NAME}?sslmode=require"

  # Always update app settings
  log "Setting app configuration..."
  SETTINGS="DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL LISTEN_ADDR=:8080 WEBSITES_PORT=8080 NO_AUTH=${NO_AUTH:-true}"
  if [[ -n "${GOOGLE_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS GOOGLE_API_KEY=$GOOGLE_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_PROVIDER:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_PROVIDER=$LLM_PROVIDER"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_MODEL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_MODEL=$LLM_MODEL"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_API_KEY=$OPENAI_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_BASE_URL=$OPENAI_BASE_URL"
  fi
  az webapp config appsettings set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --settings $SETTINGS \
    --output none

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Setup complete!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
  echo "  API:     https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net/api/v1/health"
  echo ""
  echo "  To redeploy after code changes: $0 deploy"
}

cmd_deploy() {
  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Deployed!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
}

cmd_destroy() {
  warn "This will delete ALL resources in resource group: $RG"
  read -rp "Type the resource group name to confirm: " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" != "$RG" ]]; then
    error "Aborted."
  fi

  log "Deleting resource group: $RG (this may take a few minutes)..."
  az group delete --name "$RG" --yes --no-wait

  log "Deletion initiated. Resources will be removed in the background."
}

cmd_logs() {
  log "Streaming logs from $APP_NAME..."
  az webapp log tail --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"
}

# --- Main ---
case "${1:-}" in
  setup)   cmd_setup ;;
  deploy)  cmd_deploy ;;
  destroy) cmd_destroy ;;
  logs)    cmd_logs ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {setup|deploy|destroy|logs}"
    echo ""
    echo "  setup   - Create all Azure resources and deploy (idempotent)"
    echo "  deploy  - Rebuild image and restart (after code changes)"
    echo "  destroy - Delete all Azure resources"
    echo "  logs    - Stream app logs"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

  else
    log "Creating PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
    az postgres flexible-server create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --admin-user "$DB_USER" \
      --admin-password "$DB_PASSWORD" \
      --sku-name Standard_B1ms \
      --tier Burstable \
      --version 16 \
      --public-access 0.0.0.0 \
      --output none \
      --yes
  fi

  # Database
  if resource_exists postgres-db "$RG" "$DB_SERVER" "$DB_NAME"; then
    skip "Database: $DB_NAME"
  else
    log "Creating database: $DB_NAME"
    az postgres flexible-server db create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --server-name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --database-name "$DB_NAME" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Build container image (skip if image already exists in ACR)
  if resource_exists acr-image "$ACR" "changelogue:latest"; then
    skip "Container image: changelogue:latest"
  else
    log "Building container image in ACR..."
    az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs
  fi

  # App Service plan
  if resource_exists plan "$RG" "$APP_PLAN"; then
    skip "App Service plan: $APP_PLAN"
  else
    log "Creating App Service plan: $APP_PLAN ($APP_SKU)"
    az appservice plan create \
      --name "$APP_PLAN" \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --sku "$APP_SKU" \
      --is-linux \
      --output none
  fi

  # Web App
  if resource_exists webapp "$RG" "$APP_NAME"; then
    skip "Web App: $APP_NAME"
    log "Updating container image..."
  else
    log "Creating Web App: $APP_NAME"
    az webapp create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --plan "$APP_PLAN" \
      --name "$APP_NAME" \
      --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
      --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Always update ACR credentials and container config
  log "Configuring ACR credentials for App Service..."
  ACR_PASSWORD=$(az acr credential show --name "$ACR" --query "passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
  az webapp config container set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
    --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
    --container-registry-user "$ACR" \
    --container-registry-password "$ACR_PASSWORD" \
    --output none

  DB_HOST="${DB_SERVER}.postgres.database.azure.com"
  DATABASE_URL="postgres://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST}:5432/${DB_NAME}?sslmode=require"

  # Always update app settings
  log "Setting app configuration..."
  SETTINGS="DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL LISTEN_ADDR=:8080 WEBSITES_PORT=8080 NO_AUTH=${NO_AUTH:-true}"
  if [[ -n "${GOOGLE_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS GOOGLE_API_KEY=$GOOGLE_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_PROVIDER:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_PROVIDER=$LLM_PROVIDER"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_MODEL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_MODEL=$LLM_MODEL"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_API_KEY=$OPENAI_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_BASE_URL=$OPENAI_BASE_URL"
  fi
  az webapp config appsettings set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --settings $SETTINGS \
    --output none

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Setup complete!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
  echo "  API:     https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net/api/v1/health"
  echo ""
  echo "  To redeploy after code changes: $0 deploy"
}

cmd_deploy() {
  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Deployed!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
}

cmd_destroy() {
  warn "This will delete ALL resources in resource group: $RG"
  read -rp "Type the resource group name to confirm: " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" != "$RG" ]]; then
    error "Aborted."
  fi

  log "Deleting resource group: $RG (this may take a few minutes)..."
  az group delete --name "$RG" --yes --no-wait

  log "Deletion initiated. Resources will be removed in the background."
}

cmd_logs() {
  log "Streaming logs from $APP_NAME..."
  az webapp log tail --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"
}

# --- Main ---
case "${1:-}" in
  setup)   cmd_setup ;;
  deploy)  cmd_deploy ;;
  destroy) cmd_destroy ;;
  logs)    cmd_logs ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {setup|deploy|destroy|logs}"
    echo ""
    echo "  setup   - Create all Azure resources and deploy (idempotent)"
    echo "  deploy  - Rebuild image and restart (after code changes)"
    echo "  destroy - Delete all Azure resources"
    echo "  logs    - Stream app logs"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Deploy Changelogue to Azure App Service (Container).
# Idempotent — safe to re-run at any point.
#
# Usage:
#   # First-time setup (creates all resources):
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh setup
#
#   # Redeploy after code changes:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh deploy
#
#   # Tear down everything:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh destroy
#
# Required environment variables (or set in .env):
#   AZURE_DB_PASSWORD   - PostgreSQL admin password
#
# Optional environment variables:
#   AZURE_LOCATION      - Azure region (default: westus)
#   AZURE_RG            - Resource group name (default: changelogue-res)
#   AZURE_ACR           - ACR name (default: changelogueacr)
#   AZURE_DB_SERVER     - Postgres server name (default: changelogue-db)
#   AZURE_APP_PLAN      - App Service plan name (default: changelogue-plan)
#   AZURE_APP_NAME      - Web app name (default: changelogue-app)
#   AZURE_APP_SKU       - App Service SKU (default: F1)
#   AZURE_APP_LOCATION  - App Service region, if different from AZURE_LOCATION
#   GOOGLE_API_KEY      - For AI features (optional)
#   NO_AUTH             - Skip API auth (default: true)

set -euo pipefail

# Load .env if present
if [[ -f .env ]]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
  source .env
fi

# --- Configuration ---
LOCATION="${AZURE_LOCATION:-westus}"
RG="${AZURE_RG:-changelogue-res}"
ACR="${AZURE_ACR:-changelogueacr}"
DB_SERVER="${AZURE_DB_SERVER:-changelogue-db}"
DB_NAME="changelogue"
DB_USER="pgadmin"
DB_PASSWORD="${AZURE_DB_PASSWORD:-}"
APP_PLAN="${AZURE_APP_PLAN:-changelogue-plan}"
APP_NAME="${AZURE_APP_NAME:-changelogue-app}"
APP_SKU="${AZURE_APP_SKU:-F1}"
APP_LOCATION="${AZURE_APP_LOCATION:-$LOCATION}"
IMAGE="${ACR}.azurecr.io/changelogue:latest"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log()   { echo -e "${GREEN}[+]${NC} $*"; }
warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $*"; }
skip()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[=]${NC} $* (already exists, skipping)"; }
error() { echo -e "${RED}[x]${NC} $*" >&2; exit 1; }

require_password() {
  if [[ -z "$DB_PASSWORD" ]]; then
    error "AZURE_DB_PASSWORD is required. Set it in .env or export it."
  fi
}

# Check if an Azure resource exists. Returns 0 if it does.
resource_exists() {
  local type="$1"
  shift
  case "$type" in
    rg)
      az group show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr)
      az acr show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres)
      az postgres flexible-server show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres-db)
      az postgres flexible-server db show --resource-group "$1" --server-name "$2" --database-name "$3" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    plan)
      az appservice plan show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    webapp)
      az webapp show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr-image)
      az acr repository show --name "$1" --image "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
  esac
}

# --- Commands ---

cmd_setup() {
  require_password

  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  # Resource group
  if resource_exists rg "$RG"; then
    skip "Resource group: $RG"
  else
    log "Creating resource group: $RG"
    az group create --name "$RG" --location "$LOCATION" --output none
  fi

  # Container Registry
  if resource_exists acr "$ACR"; then
    skip "Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
  else
    log "Creating Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
    az acr create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$ACR" \
      --sku Basic \
      --admin-enabled true \
      --output none
  fi

  # PostgreSQL Flexible Server
  if resource_exists postgres "$RG" "$DB_SERVER"; then
    skip "PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
  else
    log "Creating PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
    az postgres flexible-server create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --admin-user "$DB_USER" \
      --admin-password "$DB_PASSWORD" \
      --sku-name Standard_B1ms \
      --tier Burstable \
      --version 16 \
      --public-access 0.0.0.0 \
      --output none \
      --yes
  fi

  # Database
  if resource_exists postgres-db "$RG" "$DB_SERVER" "$DB_NAME"; then
    skip "Database: $DB_NAME"
  else
    log "Creating database: $DB_NAME"
    az postgres flexible-server db create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --server-name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --database-name "$DB_NAME" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Build container image (skip if image already exists in ACR)
  if resource_exists acr-image "$ACR" "changelogue:latest"; then
    skip "Container image: changelogue:latest"
  else
    log "Building container image in ACR..."
    az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs
  fi

  # App Service plan
  if resource_exists plan "$RG" "$APP_PLAN"; then
    skip "App Service plan: $APP_PLAN"
  else
    log "Creating App Service plan: $APP_PLAN ($APP_SKU)"
    az appservice plan create \
      --name "$APP_PLAN" \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --sku "$APP_SKU" \
      --is-linux \
      --output none
  fi

  # Web App
  if resource_exists webapp "$RG" "$APP_NAME"; then
    skip "Web App: $APP_NAME"
    log "Updating container image..."
  else
    log "Creating Web App: $APP_NAME"
    az webapp create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --plan "$APP_PLAN" \
      --name "$APP_NAME" \
      --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
      --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Always update ACR credentials and container config
  log "Configuring ACR credentials for App Service..."
  ACR_PASSWORD=$(az acr credential show --name "$ACR" --query "passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
  az webapp config container set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
    --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
    --container-registry-user "$ACR" \
    --container-registry-password "$ACR_PASSWORD" \
    --output none

  DB_HOST="${DB_SERVER}.postgres.database.azure.com"
  DATABASE_URL="postgres://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST}:5432/${DB_NAME}?sslmode=require"

  # Always update app settings
  log "Setting app configuration..."
  SETTINGS="DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL LISTEN_ADDR=:8080 WEBSITES_PORT=8080 NO_AUTH=${NO_AUTH:-true}"
  if [[ -n "${GOOGLE_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS GOOGLE_API_KEY=$GOOGLE_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_PROVIDER:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_PROVIDER=$LLM_PROVIDER"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_MODEL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_MODEL=$LLM_MODEL"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_API_KEY=$OPENAI_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_BASE_URL=$OPENAI_BASE_URL"
  fi
  az webapp config appsettings set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --settings $SETTINGS \
    --output none

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Setup complete!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
  echo "  API:     https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net/api/v1/health"
  echo ""
  echo "  To redeploy after code changes: $0 deploy"
}

cmd_deploy() {
  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Deployed!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
}

cmd_destroy() {
  warn "This will delete ALL resources in resource group: $RG"
  read -rp "Type the resource group name to confirm: " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" != "$RG" ]]; then
    error "Aborted."
  fi

  log "Deleting resource group: $RG (this may take a few minutes)..."
  az group delete --name "$RG" --yes --no-wait

  log "Deletion initiated. Resources will be removed in the background."
}

cmd_logs() {
  log "Streaming logs from $APP_NAME..."
  az webapp log tail --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"
}

# --- Main ---
case "${1:-}" in
  setup)   cmd_setup ;;
  deploy)  cmd_deploy ;;
  destroy) cmd_destroy ;;
  logs)    cmd_logs ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {setup|deploy|destroy|logs}"
    echo ""
    echo "  setup   - Create all Azure resources and deploy (idempotent)"
    echo "  deploy  - Rebuild image and restart (after code changes)"
    echo "  destroy - Delete all Azure resources"
    echo "  logs    - Stream app logs"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

content.text:
Now re-run with a region that has F1 quota:

```bash
AZURE_APP_LOCATION=westus3 ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh setup
```

`westus3`, `centralus`, `northcentralus`, and `canadacentral` all worked. Your Postgres stays in `westus` — the cross-region latency is negligible for this app.

message.content: why in the production, when I go to https://changelogue-app.azurewebsites.net/projects/7570ea84-abcd-4e2c-912e-99c894c41f68, it shows dashboard page?

content.text:
That's because the Next.js static export generates HTML files like `projects.html`, but there's no file at `projects/7570ea84-abcd-4e2c-912e-99c894c41f68`. So the fallback in `frontend.go` serves `index.html` (the dashboard), which doesn't know about the route.

Let me check how the frontend routing works.

input.prompt:

1. What routes/pages exist (look at page.tsx files in the app directory)
2. How dynamic routes like /projects/[id] are handled
3. Whether there's any client-side routing/navigation setup
4. Check if next.config.ts has any special routing config
5. Check if there's a catch-all route or layout that handles navigation

List all the page.tsx files and their paths, and explain how the routing is supposed to work with the static export (output: "export").

I now have a complete picture. Here is the full report. --- ## Next.js Frontend Routing in `web/app/` ### 1. All `page.tsx` Files and Their Routes | File Path | URL Route | Type | |---|---|---| | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/page.tsx` | `/` | Dashboard | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/projects/page.tsx` | `/projects` | Projects list | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx` | `/projects/:id` | Project detail | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/projects/[id]/agent/page.tsx` | `/projects/:id/agent` | Project agent view | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/projects/[id]/semantic-releases/[srId]/page.tsx` | `/projects/:id/semantic-releases/:srId` | Semantic release detail | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/releases/page.tsx` | `/releases` | All releases list | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/releases/[id]/page.tsx` | `/releases/:id` | Release detail | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/semantic-releases/page.tsx` | `/semantic-releases` | Semantic releases list | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/channels/page.tsx` | `/channels` | Notification channels | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/sources/page.tsx` | `/sources` | Sources (read-only list) | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/subscriptions/page.tsx` | `/subscriptions` | Subscriptions | | `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/layout.tsx` | (root layout, wraps all) | Shell with sidebar | --- ### 2. Dynamic Routes and How They Are Handled There are three dynamic route segments: **`/projects/[id]`** ```tsx // web/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx export async function generateStaticParams() { return [{ id: "0" }]; // placeholder only } export default async function ProjectDetailPage({ params }) { const { id } = await params; return ; } ``` **`/releases/[id]`** — same pattern, placeholder `{ id: "0" }`. **`/projects/[id]/semantic-releases/[srId]`** — two-segment dynamic route, placeholder `{ id: "0", srId: "0" }`. **`/projects/[id]/agent`** — placeholder `{ id: "0" }`. All dynamic pages use the same workaround strategy: `generateStaticParams` returns a single dummy entry `[{ id: "0" }]` (referencing [vercel/next.js#71862](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/71862)). This satisfies Next.js's requirement that static-export dynamic routes have at least one pre-rendered path. The actual content is always fetched client-side via SWR after the browser navigates to the real ID. --- ### 3. Client-Side Navigation Setup Navigation is handled by: - **`next/link`** — used throughout for all `` calls. This performs client-side SPA navigation without full page reloads. - **`next/navigation`** — `usePathname()` is used in the sidebar to determine the active nav item; `useSearchParams()` is used in `/releases/page.tsx` for the `?project=` query param filter. - **SWR (`swr`)** — every page fetches its own data client-side via `useSWR`. There is no server-side data pre-fetching; everything is `"use client"` or delegates to a client component. The sidebar (`web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx`) defines the six top-level navigation items: ```ts const navItems = [ { href: "/", label: "Dashboard", icon: LayoutDashboard }, { href: "/projects", label: "Projects", icon: FolderKanban }, { href: "/releases", label: "Releases", icon: Package }, { href: "/semantic-releases", label: "Semantic Releases", icon: Brain }, { href: "/channels", label: "Channels", icon: Megaphone }, { href: "/subscriptions", label: "Subscriptions", icon: Bell }, ]; ``` Active state is determined with `pathname.startsWith(item.href)` — so visiting `/projects/abc123` highlights the "Projects" tab. --- ### 4. `next.config.ts` — Static Export Config ```ts // web/next.config.ts const nextConfig: NextConfig = { output: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production" ? "export" : undefined, }; ``` - **Production:** `output: "export"` — Next.js emits a fully static HTML/JS bundle (no Node.js server). The Go binary serves this via `//go:embed`. - **Development:** No `output` config — runs as a normal Next.js dev server with hot reload. --- ### 5. Layout / Root Shell `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/layout.tsx` is the single root layout that wraps every page: ```tsx export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* persistent left nav */}
{children} {/* page content */}
); } ``` There is no separate catch-all route (`[...slug]`). The layout simply wraps all pages and the Sidebar is always present. --- ### How Static Export + Dynamic Routes Work Together This is the key constraint: with `output: "export"`, Next.js pre-renders pages to static HTML files at build time. Dynamic routes require `generateStaticParams` to know what pages to emit. The workaround used here (placeholder `"0"` params) means Next.js only emits `releases/0/index.html` at build time. When a user navigates to `/releases/abc123` in the browser, the static file server (the Go binary) returns the pre-built HTML shell for `/releases/[id]`. The actual data for `abc123` is then fetched client-side by SWR — the `id` prop comes from the Next.js router params hydrated client-side, not from server-rendered content. This means **all real content is runtime client-side fetched** — the static export is essentially a shell SPA, not a truly pre-rendered multi-page site.

The problem is in `frontend.go`. When you visit `/projects/7570ea84-...`, there's no matching file on disk, so it falls back to `index.html` (the dashboard). But it should fall back to the correct dynamic route's HTML shell instead.

With `output: "export"`, Next.js generates files like `projects/[id]/index.html` → `projects/0/index.html`. That HTML shell works for **any** project ID since data is fetched client-side via SWR. So the fix is: try matching known dynamic route patterns before falling back to `index.html`.

    10→// It serves index.html for any path that doesn't match a file on disk,

    12→func RegisterFrontend(mux *http.ServeMux, webDir string) {
    13→	mux.Handle("GET /", http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    14→		// Try the exact path first
    15→		path := filepath.Join(webDir, filepath.Clean(r.URL.Path))
    16→		if info, err := os.Stat(path); err == nil && !info.IsDir() {
    17→			http.ServeFile(w, r, path)
    18→			return
    19→		}
    20→		// Try path + ".html" (Next.js static export convention)
    21→		if info, err := os.Stat(path + ".html"); err == nil && !info.IsDir() {
    22→			http.ServeFile(w, r, path+".html")
    23→			return
    24→		}
    25→		// Fall back to index.html for client-side routing
    26→		http.ServeFile(w, r, filepath.Join(webDir, "index.html"))
    27→	}))

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, master]

jobs:
  backend:
    name: Backend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod

      - name: Vet
        run: go vet ./...

      - name: Build
        run: go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server

      - name: Test
        run: go test ./...

  frontend:
    name: Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Lint
        run: npx eslint

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

  integration:
    name: Integration

content.text:
Committed. To set up the GitHub Actions workflow, you'll need to add one secret to your repo:

**`AZURE_CREDENTIALS`** — create a service principal and add its JSON output:
```bash
az ad sp create-for-rbac --name "changelogue-deploy" \
  --role contributor \
  --scopes /subscriptions/<your-subscription-id>/resourceGroups/changelogue-res \
  --json-auth
```

Then add the JSON output as a secret at `Settings → Secrets → Actions → AZURE_CREDENTIALS` in your GitHub repo.

The workflow triggers on push to `main` and can also be run manually via `workflow_dispatch`.

message.content: how to set env then?

content.text:
You can set the Azure variables in two places:

## GitHub Repository Variables (for the workflow)

Go to your repo → **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions**:

**Secrets** (sensitive values):
- `AZURE_CREDENTIALS` — the service principal JSON

**Variables** (non-sensitive, optional — defaults are already in the workflow):
- `AZURE_LOCATION` — defaults to `westus`
- `AZURE_RG` — defaults to `changelogue-res`
- `AZURE_ACR` — defaults to `changelogueacr`
- `AZURE_APP_NAME` — defaults to `changelogue-app`

## App Settings (runtime env vars for the running app)

These are already set by the `setup` script. To update them later:

```bash
# Single variable
az webapp config appsettings set \
  --resource-group changelogue-res \
  --name changelogue-app \
  --settings GOOGLE_API_KEY='your-key'

# Multiple at once
az webapp config appsettings set \
  --resource-group changelogue-res \
  --name changelogue-app \
  --settings \
    LLM_PROVIDER=openai \
    OPENAI_API_KEY='your-key' \
    NO_AUTH=false
```

The app restarts automatically when settings change. No redeployment needed.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Deploy Changelogue to Azure App Service (Container).
# Idempotent — safe to re-run at any point.
#
# Usage:
#   # First-time setup (creates all resources):
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh setup
#
#   # Redeploy after code changes:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh deploy
#
#   # Tear down everything:
#   ./scripts/azure-deploy.sh destroy
#
# Required environment variables (or set in .env):
#   AZURE_DB_PASSWORD   - PostgreSQL admin password
#
# Optional environment variables:
#   AZURE_LOCATION      - Azure region (default: westus)
#   AZURE_RG            - Resource group name (default: changelogue-res)
#   AZURE_ACR           - ACR name (default: changelogueacr)
#   AZURE_DB_SERVER     - Postgres server name (default: changelogue-db)
#   AZURE_APP_PLAN      - App Service plan name (default: changelogue-plan)
#   AZURE_APP_NAME      - Web app name (default: changelogue-app)
#   AZURE_APP_SKU       - App Service SKU (default: F1)
#   AZURE_APP_LOCATION  - App Service region, if different from AZURE_LOCATION
#   GOOGLE_API_KEY      - For AI features (optional)
#   NO_AUTH             - Skip API auth (default: true)

set -euo pipefail

# Load .env if present
if [[ -f .env ]]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
  source .env
fi

# --- Configuration ---
LOCATION="${AZURE_LOCATION:-westus}"
RG="${AZURE_RG:-changelogue-res}"
ACR="${AZURE_ACR:-changelogueacr}"
DB_SERVER="${AZURE_DB_SERVER:-changelogue-db}"
DB_NAME="changelogue"
DB_USER="pgadmin"
DB_PASSWORD="${AZURE_DB_PASSWORD:-}"
APP_PLAN="${AZURE_APP_PLAN:-changelogue-plan}"
APP_NAME="${AZURE_APP_NAME:-changelogue-app}"
APP_SKU="${AZURE_APP_SKU:-F1}"
APP_LOCATION="${AZURE_APP_LOCATION:-$LOCATION}"
IMAGE="${ACR}.azurecr.io/changelogue:latest"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log()   { echo -e "${GREEN}[+]${NC} $*"; }
warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $*"; }
skip()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[=]${NC} $* (already exists, skipping)"; }
error() { echo -e "${RED}[x]${NC} $*" >&2; exit 1; }

require_password() {
  if [[ -z "$DB_PASSWORD" ]]; then
    error "AZURE_DB_PASSWORD is required. Set it in .env or export it."
  fi
}

# Check if an Azure resource exists. Returns 0 if it does.
resource_exists() {
  local type="$1"
  shift
  case "$type" in
    rg)
      az group show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr)
      az acr show --name "$1" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres)
      az postgres flexible-server show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    postgres-db)
      az postgres flexible-server db show --resource-group "$1" --server-name "$2" --database-name "$3" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    plan)
      az appservice plan show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    webapp)
      az webapp show --resource-group "$1" --name "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    acr-image)
      az acr repository show --name "$1" --image "$2" --output none 2>/dev/null
      ;;
  esac
}

# --- Commands ---

cmd_setup() {
  require_password

  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  # Resource group
  if resource_exists rg "$RG"; then
    skip "Resource group: $RG"
  else
    log "Creating resource group: $RG"
    az group create --name "$RG" --location "$LOCATION" --output none
  fi

  # Container Registry
  if resource_exists acr "$ACR"; then
    skip "Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
  else
    log "Creating Azure Container Registry: $ACR"
    az acr create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$ACR" \
      --sku Basic \
      --admin-enabled true \
      --output none
  fi

  # PostgreSQL Flexible Server
  if resource_exists postgres "$RG" "$DB_SERVER"; then
    skip "PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
  else
    log "Creating PostgreSQL Flexible Server: $DB_SERVER"
    az postgres flexible-server create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --admin-user "$DB_USER" \
      --admin-password "$DB_PASSWORD" \
      --sku-name Standard_B1ms \
      --tier Burstable \
      --version 16 \
      --public-access 0.0.0.0 \
      --output none \
      --yes
  fi

  # Database
  if resource_exists postgres-db "$RG" "$DB_SERVER" "$DB_NAME"; then
    skip "Database: $DB_NAME"
  else
    log "Creating database: $DB_NAME"
    az postgres flexible-server db create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --server-name "$DB_SERVER" \
      --database-name "$DB_NAME" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Build container image (skip if image already exists in ACR)
  if resource_exists acr-image "$ACR" "changelogue:latest"; then
    skip "Container image: changelogue:latest"
  else
    log "Building container image in ACR..."
    az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs
  fi

  # App Service plan
  if resource_exists plan "$RG" "$APP_PLAN"; then
    skip "App Service plan: $APP_PLAN"
  else
    log "Creating App Service plan: $APP_PLAN ($APP_SKU) in $APP_LOCATION"
    az appservice plan create \
      --name "$APP_PLAN" \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --sku "$APP_SKU" \
      --is-linux \
      --location "$APP_LOCATION" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Web App
  if resource_exists webapp "$RG" "$APP_NAME"; then
    skip "Web App: $APP_NAME"
    log "Updating container image..."
  else
    log "Creating Web App: $APP_NAME"
    az webapp create \
      --resource-group "$RG" \
      --plan "$APP_PLAN" \
      --name "$APP_NAME" \
      --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
      --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
      --output none
  fi

  # Always update ACR credentials and container config
  log "Configuring ACR credentials for App Service..."
  ACR_PASSWORD=$(az acr credential show --name "$ACR" --query "passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
  az webapp config container set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --container-image-name "$IMAGE" \
    --container-registry-url "https://${ACR}.azurecr.io" \
    --container-registry-user "$ACR" \
    --container-registry-password "$ACR_PASSWORD" \
    --output none

  DB_HOST="${DB_SERVER}.postgres.database.azure.com"
  DATABASE_URL="postgres://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST}:5432/${DB_NAME}?sslmode=require"

  # Always update app settings
  log "Setting app configuration..."
  SETTINGS="DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL LISTEN_ADDR=:8080 WEBSITES_PORT=8080 NO_AUTH=${NO_AUTH:-true}"
  if [[ -n "${GOOGLE_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS GOOGLE_API_KEY=$GOOGLE_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_PROVIDER:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_PROVIDER=$LLM_PROVIDER"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${LLM_MODEL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS LLM_MODEL=$LLM_MODEL"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_API_KEY=$OPENAI_API_KEY"
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-}" ]]; then
    SETTINGS="$SETTINGS OPENAI_BASE_URL=$OPENAI_BASE_URL"
  fi
  az webapp config appsettings set \
    --resource-group "$RG" \
    --name "$APP_NAME" \
    --settings $SETTINGS \
    --output none

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Setup complete!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
  echo "  API:     https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net/api/v1/health"
  echo ""
  echo "  To redeploy after code changes: $0 deploy"
}

cmd_deploy() {
  log "Checking Azure CLI login..."
  az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 || az login

  log "Building container image in ACR..."
  az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image changelogue:latest . --no-logs

  log "Restarting App Service..."
  az webapp restart --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"

  log "Deployed!"
  echo ""
  echo "  App URL: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
}

cmd_destroy() {
  warn "This will delete ALL resources in resource group: $RG"
  read -rp "Type the resource group name to confirm: " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" != "$RG" ]]; then
    error "Aborted."
  fi

  log "Deleting resource group: $RG (this may take a few minutes)..."
  az group delete --name "$RG" --yes --no-wait

  log "Deletion initiated. Resources will be removed in the background."
}

cmd_logs() {
  log "Streaming logs from $APP_NAME..."
  az webapp log tail --resource-group "$RG" --name "$APP_NAME"
}

# --- Main ---
case "${1:-}" in
  setup)   cmd_setup ;;
  deploy)  cmd_deploy ;;
  destroy) cmd_destroy ;;
  logs)    cmd_logs ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {setup|deploy|destroy|logs}"
    echo ""
    echo "  setup   - Create all Azure resources and deploy (idempotent)"
    echo "  deploy  - Rebuild image and restart (after code changes)"
    echo "  destroy - Delete all Azure resources"
    echo "  logs    - Stream app logs"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

content.text:
Pushed. Now regarding the GitHub Actions error — the `azure/login@v2` action expects the credentials in a specific JSON format. You need to create the secret correctly.

Run this locally to generate the credentials:

```bash
az ad sp create-for-rbac --name "changelogue-deploy" \
  --role contributor \
  --scopes /subscriptions/$(az account show --query id -o tsv)/resourceGroups/changelogue-res \
  --json-auth
```

This outputs JSON like:
```json
{
  "clientId": "...",
  "clientSecret": "...",
  "subscriptionId": "...",
  "tenantId": "...",
  ...
}
```

Copy the **entire JSON output** and set it as the `AZURE_CREDENTIALS` secret in your repo at:

**Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret**
- Name: `AZURE_CREDENTIALS`
- Value: *(paste the full JSON)*

Then re-run the workflow.

message.content: After the fix, releases and projects page don't go to the dashboard but shows release not found, project not found

content.text:
The issue is that when Next.js static export generates the shell HTML for dynamic routes (e.g., `projects/0.html`), the shell renders with param `"0"`, then client-side React hydrates and reads the actual URL param. But since we're serving `projects/0.html` for a different URL path, the client-side router might not pick up the correct ID from the URL.

Let me investigate how the frontend components read the route params.

input.prompt:
I need to understand why serving `projects/0.html` for `/projects/{uuid}` results in "project not found" instead of loading the correct project.

1. Read web/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx - how does it get the `id` param?
2. Read the ProjectDetail component it renders - how does it use the id?
3. Read web/app/releases/[id]/page.tsx - same question
4. Check if there's any useParams() or similar hook usage
5. Check if the components use `params` from the page props vs reading from the URL

The key question: when we serve `projects/0.html` at URL `/projects/some-uuid`, does the client-side code read `id` as `"0"` (from the page props) or as `"some-uuid"` (from the browser URL)?

content.text:
The problem is clear: the `id` is baked into the static HTML as `"0"` at build time, so the components always call the API with `id="0"` instead of the real UUID from the URL.

The fix: use `useParams()` in the client components instead of passing `id` from the static page props. Let me find and update all affected files.
Now let me read the client components to see their prop interfaces.

Now I have all the context. Let me update all four components and their page files.

fix(frontend): use useParams() for dynamic route IDs in static export

Static export bakes params at build time (always "0"), so client
components now read the real ID from the browser URL via useParams().
Fixes "not found" errors when navigating to /projects/{uuid} or
/releases/{uuid} in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin main

Redeploy with `./scripts/azure-deploy.sh deploy` to get this live.
